title: Creamy Polenta with Sage and Roasted Wild Mushrooms
ingredients: ⅔ cup crème fraîche or sour cream
1 ounce Monterey Jack cheese, shredded (about ¼ cup)
1 ounce Parmesan cheese, freshly grated (about ¼ cup)
⅛ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
3 tablespoons balsamic vinegar or red wine vinegar
1 pound large fresh wild mushrooms (shiitake, oyster, or cremini)
⅛ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
instructions: Bring water, chicken broth, and garlic to a boil in a large ovenproof saucepan.
Reduce heat to medium and cook, stirring constantly, for about 5 minutes.
Cover and place pan in oven.
Bake until thick but still creamy, stirring occasionally, about 45 minutes, adding additional water if mixture seems dry.
Heat olive oil in a small skillet over medium-high heat.
Fry sage leaves until crisp, about 10 seconds.
Drain sage on paper towels and season lightly with salt, then set aside.
Preheat oven to 425°F and line two baking sheets with foil.
Toss garlic, olive oil, vinegar, rosemary, thyme, and mushrooms in a large bowl.
Season mushroom mixture with salt and pepper.
Arrange mushrooms in a single layer on prepared baking sheets and roast until tender and slightly crisp at edges, about 25 minutes.
Stir crème fraîche, Monterey Jack, Parmesan, butter, salt, and pepper into the polenta until smooth.
Spoon polenta onto serving plates, top with roasted mushrooms, garnish with crispy sage, and serve immediately.
notes: For the polenta, add additional water during baking if the mixture seems too dry
The crispy sage leaves can be made ahead and stored in an airtight container
Roasted mushrooms should be served immediately after roasting for best texture
Wild mushroom varieties like shiitake, oyster, or cremini work best for this dish
